is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study mentions Plasmodium falciparum malaria and parasitaemia in Kenyan children.

reports_target_markers.evidence: No drug-resistance markers or codon positions are reported; this is a clinical prognostic study of capillary refill time.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Reports clinical prevalence of malaria parasitaemia, not molecular marker frequencies.

sub_country_location.evidence: Study conducted at Kilifi District Hospital, Kilifi, Kenya - a sub-national site.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Collection period 1 September 1999 to 31 December 2000 (<3 years).

african_field_samples.evidence: Kenyan children admitted to Kilifi District Hospital.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Single-site clinical dataset from Kilifi District Hospital.

supplement.evidence: No molecular marker data referenced anywhere; supplement would not help.

assessment.summary: This is a clinical prognostic study evaluating delayed capillary refill time in Kenyan children admitted to Kilifi District Hospital. It concerns malaria and P. falciparum only as clinical diagnoses, and reports no drug-resistance markers or molecular frequency data whatsoever.
exclusion_reasons: Does not report any curated P. falciparum drug-resistance marker codon positions
No extractable per-locus molecular resistance frequencies; study is about clinical capillary refill time prognosis
